notes: BUGFIX: one-time (one-off) expenses were missing from the projection sheet's Total Expenses for current and past months. ROOT CAUSE: renderBudget overlays past/current months by rebuilding Total Expenses from journal actuals — categories + EMIs only (_overlayTotalExpense[i] = catActual+EMI for past, and actual+max(0,budget-actual) across categories+EMIs for current). One-offs are neither a category nor an EMI, so the overlay never included them; they silently dropped out. Result: a current-month one-off rendered as its own 'One-time expenses' row but did NOT increase Total Expenses (verified live: engine total 212387 incl a 45000 July one-off, but the sheet showed 167387). Future months were already correct because the overlay skips them (continue on _isFuture) and keeps the engine total, which includes one-offs. THE FIX: after the past/current overlay computation, add the engine's one-off series back on top for every non-future month: _overlayTotalExpense[i] += p.oneOffExpSeries[sliceFrom + i]. Indexed with sliceFrom+i because the overlay loops over the visible window while p.oneOffExpSeries is full-horizon. User chose all months (past, current, future) for full consistency. This automatically flows into the sheet's derived surplus / residue / cash rows for those months (a one-off now correctly reduces surplus). SCOPE: this corrects the projection SHEET's display overlay only; the projection engine's own totalExpense/netWorth and projectSavingsChain already counted one-offs and are untouched, so the Net Worth page and Dashboard (which read the engine series / chain) are unaffected and remain consistent. POSSIBLE EDGE CASE (accepted): if a past one-off was ALSO logged as a separate journal expense transaction, it could count twice for that month — same behavior the engine already had for future months; not handled specially. KNOWN ITEM (still deferred): current-month savings forecast uses the engine's PLANNED surplus; touches projectSavingsChain and needs double-count verification. NOTE carried forward: Dashboard Liquidity-health card uses p.cashSeries while the hero Liquidity value uses chain-adjusted cash; unify later if they diverge. SELF_TEST.js and PROPAGATION_TEST_{1,2,3}.js remain valid; re-run to confirm invariants.
